summary: Crisp sound‑board recording of a 1985 show where the second set shines (Scarlet > Fire, West L.A., Cold Rain) but ragged vocals and sluggish playing dampen the overall vibe.
review: The SBD capture is praised for its clear, well‑balanced mix, making every nuance audible. Musically the concert is a mixed bag – the first set feels laid‑back and at times unfocused, while the second set delivers standout moments like Scarlet > Fire and an inspired West L.A., though many reviewers lament Jerry’s and Bob’s hoarse vocals and a sluggish rhythm section.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistent comments describe excellent sound‑board quality (recording ~4½/5) but the performance is viewed as average to below average due to vocal and playing issues (show ~2½/5). Weighted average yields a ~3.1 overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, well‑balanced mix; reviewers repeatedly note top‑notch SBD sound with no major drop‑outs or muddiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set feels mellow and somewhat unfocused; the second set lifts the show with strong jams and tighter transitions, highlighted by Scarlet > Fire and a flowing West L.A. sequence.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals described as ragged, "drugged‑up" or strained on many tracks, though occasional nice runs noted in Scarlet and West L.A.; overall vocal health is a concern.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s voice called out as rusty, “frog gargle,” and generally weak; some reviewers note slight improvement during the Mama Tried > Big River duet.
band_member_comments.Drums: Comments about sluggish 80s drumming and an indifferent, thudding feel that slows the momentum.
